basic_details.job_title: OSSC FSO Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 84 Food Safety Officer Posts
basic_details.department: Office of Commissioner of Food Safety, Odisha, BBSR (Health & Family Welfare Department)
basic_details.short_information: OSSC is offering for 84 Food Safety Officer posts with qualifications B.Sc, B.Tech/B.E, M.Sc, M.Phil/Ph.D, last date 11 July 2026

important_dates.preliminary examination date: To be notified on OSSC website

application_fee.details: Candidates are required to pay the examination fee through the online mode only via the Odisha Treasury portal linked with the online application form. Fee once paid will be refunded only to candidates who actually appear in the first stage (Preliminary Examination) of the recruitment. Applications without payment of examination fees (except SC/ST/PwD candidates) will be treated as incomplete and liable for rejection. All candidates must clearly mention their valid/active bank account details in the online application for receiving the refundable fee.

age_limit.details: Candidates must be minimum 21 years and maximum 42 years as on 01-01-2025. The upper age limit is relaxable by 5 years for SEBC, SC, ST, and all Women candidates, and by 10 years for PwD candidates. PwD candidates belonging to SC/ST category are entitled to a cumulative relaxation of 10 years over and above the normal relaxation for their category. Candidates falling under more than one category shall be eligible for only one benefit of age relaxation, whichever is most beneficial. | Note: To be eligible, candidates not enjoying any relaxation must not have been born earlier than 2nd January 1983 and not later than 1st January 2004.

qualification.required_degree: Bachelor's Degree in Food Technology / Dairy Technology / Bio Technology / Agricultural Science / Oil Technology / Veterinary Science / Bio-Chemistry / Microbiology from a recognized University or institution. OR Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery / Bachelor of Dental Surgery / Bachelor of Homeopathic Medicine and Surgery / Bachelor of Unani Medicine and Surgery / Bachelor of Siddha Medicine and Surgery / Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery / Bachelor of Sowa Rigpa Medicine and Surgery from a recognized University.
qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the last date of submission of Online Application Form. Candidates must be a citizen of India, of good character, of sound health and free from organic defects, able to read, write and speak Odia fluently (with proof via Middle School/Matriculation/Class-VII exam with Odia as subject). If married, must not have more than one spouse living. | Educational Qualification

The selection process for OSSC Food Safety Officer Recruitment 2026 consists of 3 (three) successive stages – Stage I: Preliminary Examination (150 marks
CBT/OMR
150 minutes)
Stage II: Main Written Examination (200 marks
Technical Paper) + Computer Skill Test (qualifying
50% marks required)

and Stage III: Certificate Verification. Candidates failing to appear at any stage will be rejected and will not be included in the merit list. The Commission at its discretion may conduct the examination through OMR or CBRE mode.
Stage I – Preliminary Examination
Candidates numbering a minimum of 5 times of the vacancies (post-wise and category-wise) shall be shortlisted for the Main Written Examination based on marks in Preliminary Examination.
Stage II (a) – Main Written Examination (Technical Paper)
Stage II (b) – Computer Skill Test
Stage III – Certificate Verification

Candidates numbering 1.5 times of the vacancies advertised (post-wise and category-wise) shall be shortlisted for Certificate Verification based on marks in the Main Written Examination (Technical Paper) and having qualified the Computer Skill Test.
Tie-Breaking Rules

syllabus.details: The Preliminary Examination is based on a general indicative syllabus (Annexure-C). The Main Written Examination Technical Paper syllabus for Food Safety Officer is given in Annexure-D (Section II) of the official notification. All papers are MCQ-based, bilingual (Odia and English), except Paper I of the Main Exam (language). The exam medium option once given in the online application cannot be changed. | Preliminary Examination – Indicative Syllabus (Annexure-C) | Computer Skill Test – Indicative Syllabus (Annexure-C) | Main Written Examination – Technical Paper Syllabus for Food Safety Officer (Annexure-D, Section II)

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only through the OSSC official website https://www.ossc.gov.in. No physical/hard copy application will be accepted. New users must click on "NEW USER" button and complete Online Registration. Registered users must click on "REGISTERED USER" and proceed to fill the application form. The candidate can exercise options for more than one post/service as per their eligibility. Only one online application is allowed per candidate; multiple applications will result in cancellation of all applications. Candidates are strongly advised to submit the application well in advance without waiting for the closing date.

Candidates must upload valid Certificate and Marksheet(s) of qualifying degree at the time of online application. SC/ST/SEBC category candidates must submit valid online caste certificate details. Government servants/PSU employees must upload NOC at the time of application. PwD candidates must upload permanent disability certificate issued by UDID. After submission of the final online application, no change/correction/modification will be allowed under any circumstances.
Documents to be Submitted at the Time of Certificate Verification:
Downloaded copy of Admission Letter for Certificate Verification
Downloaded copy of Bio-Data-cum-Attestation form duly filled and signed
Copy of Online Application form legibly signed by the candidate
HSC Certificate & Mark Sheet (for Date of Birth proof)
Bachelor's Degree Certificate & Mark sheets / Master's Degree / Doctorate Degree Certificate & Mark sheets (as applicable)
Computer Certificate & Marksheet (as applicable for the post)
Caste certificate issued by competent authority (SC/ST/SEBC candidates); SEBC certificate must be within one year prior to closing date
Certificate of passing HSC with Odia as compulsory subject or certificate of passing Odia of M.E. standard
Photocopy of permanent disability certificate issued by UDID (for PwD candidates)
Discharge certificate, identity card, and service period document (for Ex-Servicemen candidates)
Sports Identity Card issued by Director of Sports & Youth Service Department, Govt. of Odisha (for Sports Person candidates)
NOC or Standing NOC (for candidates working in Government Service/PSUs)
Government-issued ID Proof (Aadhaar Card / PAN Card / Voter's Identity Card / Driving License etc.)
important_instructions: Only bonafide Indian citizens are eligible to apply; must be able to read, write and speak Odia fluently. Candidates must possess the requisite qualification on the last date of submission of Online Application Form. No hard copy / physical application will be accepted – online submission only. Examination fee is refundable only to candidates who actually appear in the Preliminary Examination. Only one online application is allowed per candidate; multiple applications will result in cancellation. Candidates applying for Senior Economic Investigator post must choose one of 13 technical paper subjects in the online form; this choice is final and cannot be changed. PwD candidates must have permanent disability of not below 40% to be eligible to apply under PwD category. PwD candidates requiring scribe must opt for scribe in the online application form; scribe facility is not allowed for Computer Skill Test. Government servants / PSU employees must obtain and upload NOC from their controlling authority; those who become Government servants after submission must produce NOC at Certificate Verification. Mobile phones and electronic gadgets are banned inside examination premises. No Admission Letter will be sent by post; candidates must download from OSSC website regularly. All information published regarding this recruitment will be on the OSSC website only – candidates are advised to check it regularly. Any misrepresentation/use of forged/false documents at any stage of selection will result in cancellation of candidature, debarment, and criminal proceedings. There shall be no provision for re-evaluation/re-checking of scores; no correspondence in this regard shall be entertained. All decisions of the Commission regarding eligibility, selection, and other matters are final and binding.

content_sections.heading: OSSC FSO Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: A total of 84 vacancies have been announced for the post of Food Safety Officer (Group-B) under the Office of Commissioner of Food Safety, Odisha. Vacancies are distributed across UR, SEBC, SC, and ST categories with women's horizontal reservation included. PwD (Persons with Disability) Sub-Category Vacancy (Total 06 Posts = out of which W-02) Special Category (Ex-Servicemen & Sports Person) Note: Candidates belonging to the Transgender community are also eligible to apply. The number of vacancies is subject to change without prior notice at the discretion of the Commission, the Requisitioning Authorities, or the Government.

tables.caption: PwD (Persons with Disability) Sub-Category Vacancy (Total 06 Posts = out of which W-02)

rows.benchmark_disability_eligible: Cat-II: Hard of Hearing with Suitable Aid

rows.no_of_vacancies: Cat-III: Dwarfism, Acid attack victim, Leprosy Cured

content_sections.heading: OSSC FSO Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the last date of submission of Online Application Form. Candidates must be a citizen of India, of good character, of sound health and free from organic defects, able to read, write and speak Odia fluently (with proof via Middle School/Matriculation/Class-VII exam with Odia as subject). If married, must not have more than one spouse living. Educational Qualification

rows.details: Bachelor's Degree in Food Technology / Dairy Technology / Bio Technology / Agricultural Science / Oil Technology / Veterinary Science / Bio-Chemistry / Microbiology from a recognized University or institution. OR Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery / Bachelor of Dental Surgery / Bachelor of Homeopathic Medicine and Surgery / Bachelor of Unani Medicine and Surgery / Bachelor of Siddha Medicine and Surgery / Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery / Bachelor of Sowa Rigpa Medicine and Surgery from a recognized University.

Master's Degree in Food Technology / Dairy Technology / Bio Technology / Agricultural Science / Oil Technology / Veterinary Science / Bio-Chemistry / Microbiology / Chemistry from a recognized University or institution.

Doctorate Degree in Food Technology / Dairy Technology / Bio Technology / Agricultural Science / Oil Technology / Veterinary Science / Bio-Chemistry / Microbiology from a recognized University or institution.
Must possess adequate knowledge in Computer Application.

Food Engineering; Food Engineering & Technology; Food Processing & Preservation; Food Processing Technology; Food Technology & Management; Food Biotechnology; Food Plant Operations Management; Food Process Engineering & Management; Food Processing; Food Safety & Quality Management; Process & Food Engineering.
Agriculture; Agricultural Engineering; Fisheries; Food Nutrition and Dietetic; Horticulture.

content_sections.heading: OSSC FSO Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit
content_sections.text: Candidates must be minimum 21 years and maximum 42 years as on 01-01-2025. The upper age limit is relaxable by 5 years for SEBC, SC, ST, and all Women candidates, and by 10 years for PwD candidates. PwD candidates belonging to SC/ST category are entitled to a cumulative relaxation of 10 years over and above the normal relaxation for their category. Candidates falling under more than one category shall be eligible for only one benefit of age relaxation, whichever is most beneficial. Note: To be eligible, candidates not enjoying any relaxation must not have been born earlier than 2nd January 1983 and not later than 1st January 2004.

rows.age_limit_as_on_01_01_2025: 10 years relaxation over applicable category limit

content_sections.heading: OSSC FSO Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee
content_sections.text: Candidates are required to pay the examination fee through the online mode only via the Odisha Treasury portal linked with the online application form. Fee once paid will be refunded only to candidates who actually appear in the first stage (Preliminary Examination) of the recruitment. Applications without payment of examination fees (except SC/ST/PwD candidates) will be treated as incomplete and liable for rejection. All candidates must clearly mention their valid/active bank account details in the online application for receiving the refundable fee.

rows.category: General / SEBC / Women / Ex-Servicemen / Sports Person

content_sections.heading: OSSC FSO Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process
content_sections.text: The selection process for OSSC Food Safety Officer Recruitment 2026 consists of 3 (three) successive stages – Stage I: Preliminary Examination (150 marks, CBT/OMR, 150 minutes), Stage II: Main Written Examination (200 marks, CBT/OMR, Technical Paper) + Computer Skill Test (qualifying, 50% marks required), and Stage III: Certificate Verification. Candidates failing to appear at any stage will be rejected and will not be included in the merit list. The Commission at its discretion may conduct the examination through OMR or CBRE mode. Stage I – Preliminary Examination Candidates numbering a minimum of 5 times of the vacancies (post-wise and category-wise) shall be shortlisted for the Main Written Examination based on marks in Preliminary Examination. Stage II (a) – Main Written Examination (Technical Paper) Stage II (b) – Computer Skill Test Stage III – Certificate Verification Candidates numbering 1.5 times of the vacancies advertised (post-wise and category-wise) shall be shortlisted for Certificate Verification based on marks in the Main Written Examination (Technical Paper) and having qualified the Computer Skill Test. Tie-Breaking Rules

content_sections.heading: OSSC FSO Recruitment 2026 - Exam Syllabus
content_sections.text: The Preliminary Examination is based on a general indicative syllabus (Annexure-C). The Main Written Examination Technical Paper syllabus for Food Safety Officer is given in Annexure-D (Section II) of the official notification. All papers are MCQ-based, bilingual (Odia and English), except Paper I of the Main Exam (language). The exam medium option once given in the online application cannot be changed. Preliminary Examination – Indicative Syllabus (Annexure-C) Computer Skill Test – Indicative Syllabus (Annexure-C) Main Written Examination – Technical Paper Syllabus for Food Safety Officer (Annexure-D, Section II)

rows.topic: Data Interpretation (Chart, Graph, Table, Data Sufficiency etc.) – 10th Standard
Logical Reasoning and Analytical Ability, General Mental Ability
Current Events of National and International Importance
Usage of the Internet, Services available on the Internet

tables.caption: Main Written Examination – Technical Paper Syllabus for Food Safety Officer (Annexure-D, Section II)

rows.section: A. Food Safety Standards, Act, Rules & Regulations
rows.topics: Food definitions, food additives, processing aids, contaminants, toxic substances, heavy metals; pesticides; genetically modified / organic / functional foods; packaging and labelling; advertisement restrictions; responsibilities of Food Business Operators; liability of manufacturers, packers, distributors, sellers; food recall procedures; licensing and registration; GMP, GHP, HACCP/personal hygiene; water pollution and food-borne diseases.

Carbohydrates, Proteins, Lipids – definition, classification, properties, significance; essential and non-essential amino acids; vitamins (fat soluble and water soluble); enzymes – definition, classifications, properties, co-enzymes; chemical components of foods (micro and macronutrients); nutrition deficiency disorders; acid value, iodine value, saponification value of lipids; food spoilage (microorganisms, conditions, factors & control); food poisoning (intoxication) – bacterial, fungal, algal; food preservation (storage, physical and chemical methods); fermentation – types, production of cheese, yoghurt, bread.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for OSSC FSO Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Applications must be submitted online only through the OSSC official website https://www.ossc.gov.in. No physical/hard copy application will be accepted. New users must click on "NEW USER" button and complete Online Registration. Registered users must click on "REGISTERED USER" and proceed to fill the application form. The candidate can exercise options for more than one post/service as per their eligibility. Only one online application is allowed per candidate; multiple applications will result in cancellation of all applications. Candidates are strongly advised to submit the application well in advance without waiting for the closing date. Candidates must upload valid Certificate and Marksheet(s) of qualifying degree at the time of online application. SC/ST/SEBC category candidates must submit valid online caste certificate details. Government servants/PSU employees must upload NOC at the time of application. PwD candidates must upload permanent disability certificate issued by UDID. After submission of the final online application, no change/correction/modification will be allowed under any circumstances. Documents to be Submitted at the Time of Certificate Verification: Downloaded copy of Admission Letter for Certificate Verification Downloaded copy of Bio-Data-cum-Attestation form duly filled and signed Copy of Online Application form legibly signed by the candidate HSC Certificate & Mark Sheet (for Date of Birth proof) +2/equivalent certificate & mark sheet (as applicable) Bachelor's Degree Certificate & Mark sheets / Master's Degree / Doctorate Degree Certificate & Mark sheets (as applicable) Computer Certificate & Marksheet (as applicable for the post) Caste certificate issued by competent authority (SC/ST/SEBC candidates); SEBC certificate must be within one year prior to closing date Certificate of passing HSC with Odia as compulsory subject or certificate of passing Odia of M.E. standard Photocopy of permanent disability certificate issued by UDID (for PwD candidates) Discharge certificate, identity card, and service period document (for Ex-Servicemen candidates) Sports Identity Card issued by Director of Sports & Youth Service Department, Govt. of Odisha (for Sports Person candidates) NOC or Standing NOC (for candidates working in Government Service/PSUs) Government-issued ID Proof (Aadhaar Card / PAN Card / Voter's Identity Card / Driving License etc.) Apply Online at: https://www.ossc.gov.in

content_sections.text: Only bonafide Indian citizens are eligible to apply; must be able to read, write and speak Odia fluently. Candidates must possess the requisite qualification on the last date of submission of Online Application Form. No hard copy / physical application will be accepted – online submission only. Examination fee is refundable only to candidates who actually appear in the Preliminary Examination. Only one online application is allowed per candidate; multiple applications will result in cancellation. Candidates applying for Senior Economic Investigator post must choose one of 13 technical paper subjects in the online form; this choice is final and cannot be changed. PwD candidates must have permanent disability of not below 40% to be eligible to apply under PwD category. PwD candidates requiring scribe must opt for scribe in the online application form; scribe facility is not allowed for Computer Skill Test. Government servants / PSU employees must obtain and upload NOC from their controlling authority; those who become Government servants after submission must produce NOC at Certificate Verification. Mobile phones and electronic gadgets are banned inside examination premises. No Admission Letter will be sent by post; candidates must download from OSSC website regularly. All decisions of the Commission regarding eligibility, selection, and other matters are final and binding.

faq.question: Q1. What is OSSC Food Safety Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: OSSC Food Safety Officer Recruitment 2026 is a recruitment drive by Odisha Staff Selection Commission under Advertisement No. IIE-46/2025/5919/OSSC to fill 84 Group-B Food Safety Officer posts under the Office of Commissioner of Food Safety, Health & Family Welfare Department, Government of Odisha.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in OSSC FSO 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 84 vacancies are available for Food Safety Officer posts distributed as UR-44, SEBC-08, SC-13, ST-19, with 17 seats reserved for women and 6 seats for PwD candidates across categories.

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply for OSSC Food Safety Officer 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for online application submission is 11 July 2026, with online registration opening on 09 June 2026 and an editing window available till 14 July 2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility for OSSC Food Safety Officer 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have a Bachelor's/Master's/Doctorate degree in Food Technology, Dairy Technology, Biotechnology, Agricultural Science, Oil Technology, Veterinary Science, Biochemistry, Microbiology, Chemistry, or medical degrees (MBBS, BDS, BHMS, BUMS, BSMS, BAMS, BSRMS) from recognized universities, plus adequate computer knowledge.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for OSSC FSO 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 21 to 42 years as on 01 January 2025, with 5 years relaxation for SEBC/SC/ST/Women and 10 years relaxation for PwD candidates, plus additional relaxation for Ex-Servicemen.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for OSSC Food Safety Officer 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process includes Stage-I Preliminary Examination (150 marks, 150 questions, 150 minutes), Stage-II Main Written Examination Technical Paper (200 marks, 100 questions) plus Computer Skill Test (qualifying at 50%), and Stage-III Certificate Verification, with negative marking of 0.25 and 0.5 marks respectively.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for OSSC FSO 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, examination fee is ₹500 for General/SEBC/Women/Ex-Servicemen/Sports Person candidates (refundable to those who appear in Preliminary Examination), while SC/ST/PwD candidates are exempted from fee payment.

faq.question: Q8. What is the salary for OSSC Food Safety Officer 2026?
faq.answer: Food Safety Officers will receive Pay Matrix Level-9, Cell-1 as per ORSP (Odisha Revised Pay Structure) 2017 on a regular appointment basis as Group-B State Cadre officers.

faq.question: Q9. Is Odia language mandatory for OSSC Food Safety Officer 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates must be able to read, write, and speak Odia fluently and must have proof of passing Middle School/Matriculation/Class-VII examination with Odia as a subject, or ME standard Odia test conducted by School and Mass Education Department.

faq.question: Q10. How to apply for OSSC Food Safety Officer 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply online only through the official OSSC website ossc.gov.in from 09 June 2026 to 11 July 2026 by completing registration, uploading educational certificates, computer knowledge proof, caste/disability certificates (if applicable), paying examination fee, and submitting the application form.

acceptedAnswer.text: OSSC Food Safety Officer Recruitment 2026 is a recruitment drive by Odisha Staff Selection Commission under Advertisement No. IIE-46/2025/5919/OSSC to fill 84 Group-B Food Safety Officer posts under the Office of Commissioner of Food Safety, Health & Family Welfare Department, Government of Odisha.

mainEntity.name: How many vacancies are available in OSSC FSO 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: A total of 84 vacancies are available for Food Safety Officer posts distributed as UR-44, SEBC-08, SC-13, ST-19, with 17 seats reserved for women and 6 seats for PwD candidates across categories.

mainEntity.name: What is the last date to apply for OSSC Food Safety Officer 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The last date for online application submission is 11 July 2026, with online registration opening on 09 June 2026 and an editing window available till 14 July 2026.

mainEntity.name: What is the eligibility for OSSC Food Safety Officer 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Candidates must have a Bachelor's/Master's/Doctorate degree in Food Technology, Dairy Technology, Biotechnology, Agricultural Science, Oil Technology, Veterinary Science, Biochemistry, Microbiology, Chemistry, or medical degrees (MBBS, BDS, BHMS, BUMS, BSMS, BAMS, BSRMS) from recognized universities, plus adequate computer knowledge.

mainEntity.name: What is the age limit for OSSC FSO 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Candidates must be between 21 to 42 years as on 01 January 2025, with 5 years relaxation for SEBC/SC/ST/Women and 10 years relaxation for PwD candidates, plus additional relaxation for Ex-Servicemen.

mainEntity.name: What is the selection process for OSSC Food Safety Officer 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The selection process includes Stage-I Preliminary Examination (150 marks, 150 questions, 150 minutes), Stage-II Main Written Examination Technical Paper (200 marks, 100 questions) plus Computer Skill Test (qualifying at 50%), and Stage-III Certificate Verification, with negative marking of 0.25 and 0.5 marks respectively.

mainEntity.name: Is there any application fee for OSSC FSO 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, examination fee is ₹500 for General/SEBC/Women/Ex-Servicemen/Sports Person candidates (refundable to those who appear in Preliminary Examination), while SC/ST/PwD candidates are exempted from fee payment.

mainEntity.name: What is the salary for OSSC Food Safety Officer 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Food Safety Officers will receive Pay Matrix Level-9, Cell-1 as per ORSP (Odisha Revised Pay Structure) 2017 on a regular appointment basis as Group-B State Cadre officers.

mainEntity.name: Is Odia language mandatory for OSSC Food Safety Officer 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, candidates must be able to read, write, and speak Odia fluently and must have proof of passing Middle School/Matriculation/Class-VII examination with Odia as a subject, or ME standard Odia test conducted by School and Mass Education Department.

mainEntity.name: How to apply for OSSC Food Safety Officer 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Candidates must apply online only through the official OSSC website ossc.gov.in from 09 June 2026 to 11 July 2026 by completing registration, uploading educational certificates, computer knowledge proof, caste/disability certificates (if applicable), paying examination fee, and submitting the application form.
